Analytics

Analytics, Reviews & Reports

How to read platform performance metrics in Business Analytics, moderate customer reviews, and generate commission reports with order-level exports.

Platform Hyperzod Admin
Section Analytics
Version 1.0
Updated March 2026
← Hub Overview Business Analytics Reviews Reports Order Export
Analytics Overview

The Analytics Section

Navigate to Analytics in the left sidebar. It expands to reveal three sub-sections: Business Analytics, Reviews, and Reports.

SECTION 01
📊
Business Analytics
Platform-wide charts for Total Orders, Total Sales, Total Delivery Fee, Total Customers, and a Platform Overview split by iOS / Android / Web.
SECTION 02
⭐
Reviews
View all customer order reviews. Filter by merchant and date range, open order detail panels, and delete inappropriate reviews. Toggle the review feature on/off globally.
SECTION 03
📄
Reports
Commission Reports summarising merchant sales, orders, and commission earned. Filter by merchant and date. Drill into Merchant Reports for order-level detail. Export order data to CSV.
📅
Date range picker — all three sections share the same date picker in the top-right corner. Quick presets: Today, Yesterday, This month, Last month, This year. Or pick a custom range using the dual-month calendar, then click Apply.
Analytics → Business Analytics

Business Analytics

A read-only dashboard showing platform-level performance metrics across a chosen date range. No edits can be made here — it is purely for monitoring.

💡
Navigation: Analytics → Business Analytics
Total Orders
0
Broken down into: Active · Completed · Cancelled orders (colour-coded dots)
Total Sales
£0
Total revenue across all merchants for the selected period
Total Delivery Fee
£0
Sum of all delivery fees collected in the period
Total Customers
0
Split into: Existing customers (blue) · New customers (yellow)
Business Analytics — four metric panels: Total Orders (with order type breakdown), Total Sales, Total Delivery Fee, and Total Customers (existing vs new)
Metric Panel Details
Total Orders ℹ
Shows total order count for the period with a line chart. Colour-coded legend: Blue = Active orders, Green = Completed orders, Yellow = Cancelled orders. Hover over the chart to see day-by-day values.
Total Sales ℹ
Total gross revenue (£) for the selected period. Displayed with a trend line chart.
Total Delivery Fee ℹ
Sum of all delivery fees collected. Shown with a trend line chart.
Total Customers ℹ
Total unique customers who placed at least one order. Blue = Existing customers (returning). Yellow = New customers (first order in period).
ℹ info icons
Each metric card has an ℹ icon — hover to see a tooltip explaining exactly what is counted in that metric.
Date picker — select a preset (Today / Yesterday / This month / Last month / This year) or use the dual calendar to pick a custom range. Click Apply to update all charts.
Platform Overview
iOS
Number of orders placed via the iOS app in the selected period.
Android
Number of orders placed via the Android app.
Web
Number of orders placed via the web storefront.
Platform Overview at the bottom of the Business Analytics page — shows order counts split by iOS, Android, and Web for the selected date range.
💡
Tip: Use This month as the default view for a quick snapshot of current trading performance. Use Last month for month-on-month comparisons. All values update instantly when you change the date range.
Analytics → Reviews

Reviews

View all customer order reviews submitted across the platform. Filter by merchant or date range, inspect the underlying order, and delete inappropriate or irrelevant reviews.

💡
Navigation: Analytics → Reviews
Reviews List — Columns
Rating & Review
Star rating (1–5 ★) and any written review text submitted by the customer (e.g. "Fantastic", "Fire").
Order ID
The order number this review is linked to. Click the row to open the full order detail panel.
Customer Name
Name of the customer who submitted the review.
Merchant Name
The merchant the review is for.
Date & Time
When the review was submitted (e.g. Tue 20 Jan, 11:07 PM).
Actions
🗑️ Delete button — permanently removes the review. There is no edit option; reviews can only be deleted.
Reviews list — columns: Rating & Review, Order ID, Customer Name, Merchant Name, Date & Time, Actions. The Enable Order Review toggle (top right, blue = ON) controls whether customers can submit reviews platform-wide.
Filters
Search Merchants
Filter the reviews list to show only reviews for a specific merchant. Select from the dropdown, then click Search.
Select Date (top right)
Filter reviews by date range using presets or a custom calendar range. Click Apply to update the list.
Reviews date filter — same dual-calendar picker as Business Analytics. Presets: Today, Yesterday, This month, Last month, This year.
Order Detail Panel
How to open
Click anywhere on a review row to open the order detail slide-out panel on the right.
Panel tabs
Order · Timeline · Customer — same panel as seen in Managing Orders.
Order tab content
Shows: Order number & status · Delivery/pickup type · Pick and Drop address · Delivery By (e.g. Autozod) · Payment method · Source platform (e.g. iOS) · Star rating · Bill details with product list and quantities.
Review field
The star rating appears in the Order tab under Review: — useful for confirming which review belongs to which order.
Clicking a review row opens the full order detail panel. The Order tab shows delivery route, payment method, source platform, star rating, and bill details.
Enable Order Review Toggle
Location
Top-right of the Reviews page, labelled Enable Order Review.
Blue (ON)
Customers can submit reviews after completing an order. Reviews appear in this list.
Grey (OFF)
The review prompt is hidden from customers. No new reviews can be submitted. Existing reviews are still visible in the admin.
⚠️
Deleting a review is permanent. There is no confirmation prompt and no way to restore a deleted review. Only delete reviews that are clearly inappropriate, spam, or submitted in error.
Analytics → Reports

Reports

Commission Reports show a summary of each merchant's orders, revenue, and commission earned for a selected date range. Filter by one or more merchants, export the commission data, or drill into a merchant for order-level detail.

💡
Navigation: Analytics → Reports
Reports main view — four summary stat cards at the top (Total Sales, Total Commission, Total Orders, Cancelled Orders), followed by the Commission Reports table.
Total Sales
£32.47
Gross revenue across all merchants in the period
Total Commission
£2.48
Platform commission earned across all merchants
Total Orders
5
All completed orders in the period
Cancelled Orders
0
Orders cancelled within the period
Commission Reports Table
Merchant
Merchant name — click to open the Merchant Reports page for order-level detail for that merchant.
Orders
Number of orders placed at this merchant in the selected period.
Grand Total
Total revenue (£) generated by this merchant in the period.
Commission
Platform commission (£) earned from this merchant based on the configured commission rate.
Export button
Downloads the Commission Reports table as a CSV file for the current filters and date range.
Filtering
Search Merchants dropdown
Click the dropdown to see all merchants. Select one or use Select All to include all merchants. Multiple merchants can be selected simultaneously.
Search button
After selecting merchants, click Search to apply the filter and refresh the stats and table.
Date range (top right)
Change the date range using the date picker. All stats and the table update to reflect the new range.
Merchant filter dropdown — shows all merchants with Select All option. Choose one or more merchants and click Search to filter the commission table.
Date range picker on the Reports page — select a preset or custom range and click Apply. The summary stats and commission table both update immediately.
Reports → Drill-down

Merchant Reports & Order Export

Drill into a specific merchant for order-level commission detail, or export all order data for the current date range to a downloadable CSV.

Merchant Reports (order-level detail)
How to access
In the Commission Reports table, click the merchant name (shown as a blue link). This opens the Merchant Reports page for that merchant.
Order ID
Individual order number (e.g. #1075).
Date
The date the order was placed.
Grand Total
Total order value (£) including all items.
Commission
Commission charged on this specific order (£). Orders showing £0 commission may be from a merchant with a 0% commission rate or a commission-exempt order type.
Back button
Click ← in the top-left to return to the main Reports page.
Merchant Reports — shows each order for the selected merchant with Order ID, Date, Grand Total, and Commission. Click ← to return to the main Reports page.
Order Export
How to access
From the main Reports page, click the Export button (top right of the Commission Reports table). This opens the Order Export page.
Step 1 — Export
Click the Export button in the dashed box. The system queues an export job for all orders matching the current date range and merchant filters.
Step 2 — Download
Once the export job completes (Status = Completed), click the download icon (↓) in the Download column to save the file.
Export history table
Shows all previous exports with: Date, Export ID, Number of Records, Status, and Download link. Completed exports can be re-downloaded at any time from this table.
Status
Completed — export is ready to download. Other statuses may show Processing while the export is being generated.
Order Export — click Export to queue a new export, then download from the table once Status shows Completed. Previous exports are listed chronologically with their Export ID and record count.
📥
Re-downloading exports: All past completed exports remain available in the history table. You don't need to run a new export if you need data from a previous date range — just locate the relevant row and click the download icon again.
Yolulo
